title: Work and careers
explanation:
Work and careers

  • workplace: deadline, shift, promotion, contract, training
  • people: applicant, employer, colleague, manager
  • verbs: apply, resign, negotiate, supervise

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: She got a ____, so now she manages a bigger team. | *promotion | deadline | contract
questions.feedback: A promotion is a move to a higher position.

questions.text: I work the night ____, from 10 p.m. to 6 a.m. | *shift | training | salary
questions.feedback: A shift is a set working period.

questions.text: He missed the project ____, and the client was unhappy. | *deadline | promotion | office
questions.feedback: A deadline is the latest time to finish something.

questions.text: You should ____ for the role before Friday. | *apply | retire | supervise

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: She signed a two-year ____ with the company. | contract
Our ____ wants the report by noon. | manager
The company offers safety ____ for new staff. | training
He decided to ____ because he had found a better job. | resign
The best ____ had relevant experience and strong references. | applicant

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The ____ offered her a flexible schedule. | *employer | employee | applicant
He earns a good ____, but he works long hours. | *salary | shift | vacancy
A job ____ appeared on the company website this morning. | *vacancy | deadline | supervisor
My closest ____ helped me learn the new system. | *colleague | employer | contract
